edges.description: A susceptible cervical arterial wall is more likely to dissect after mechanical stress or spontaneous mural injury.
edges.target: Arterial Wall Tear and Intramural Hematoma

edges.description: The dissected arterial wall and altered lumen promote local thrombosis, stenosis, occlusion, and embolization.
edges.source: Arterial Wall Tear and Intramural Hematoma

edges.description: Molecular stress and remodeling signatures may contribute to arterial-wall vulnerability and impaired repair.
edges.source: Molecular Stress and Vascular Remodeling Signatures
edges.target: Arterial Wall Tear and Intramural Hematoma

nodes.description: Multifactorial arterial-wall susceptibility, including connective-tissue dysplasia, systemic vasculopathy, and rare monogenic connective-tissue disease, lowers the threshold for cervical arterial wall injury.

evidence.snippet: The clinical, radiological, and histological characteristics of systemic vasculopathy and undifferentiated connective tissue dysplasia are present in up to 70% of individuals with sporadic CeAD.
Monogenic heritable connective tissue diseases account for fewer than 5% of cases of CeAD.

nodes.description: A tear in the intima or bleeding within the arterial wall separates vessel wall layers, creating an intramural hematoma that narrows the lumen or forms a pseudoaneurysm.
nodes.id: Arterial Wall Tear and Intramural Hematoma

evidence.snippet: We included patients with extracranial carotid and vertebral dissection with onset of symptoms within the past 7 days.

In the acute and subacute stage, the typical sign detection of intramural hematoma and Grade II enhancement revealed by HR-MRI was higher than the observations in the chronic stage (P = 0.000/0.000/0.016), while there was no significant difference by MRA (P = 0.902).

nodes.description: Peripheral-blood transcriptomic changes in CeAD implicate molecular stress, senescence-associated signaling, mitochondrial dysfunction, and epithelial-mesenchymal plasticity as candidate mechanisms that may accompany arterial-wall vulnerability and remodeling.
nodes.id: Molecular Stress and Vascular Remodeling Signatures

evidence.snippet: We found potential correlations between CeAD and the dysregulation of genes linked to nucleolar stress, senescence-associated secretory phenotype, mitochondrial malfunction, and epithelial-mesenchymal plasticity.

nodes.description: Damaged arterial endothelium and disturbed flow promote local thrombosis and embolization to cerebral arteries.

evidence.snippet: Extracranial carotid and vertebral artery dissection is an important cause of stroke, especially in young people.
